body:
Moves the prognostic LAI model from Zhou et al. (2025) out of `pmodel.jl` into a new `optimal_lai.jl` module following the canopy component pattern, and integrates it as a full CanopyModel component with callback support.

## Changes

**New module: `src/standalone/Vegetation/optimal_lai.jl`**
- `AbstractLAIModel{FT}` - Base type for LAI models
- `OptimalLAIModel{FT}` - Concrete implementation with parameters `k`, `z`, `chi`, `f0`, `sigma`, `alpha`
- Auxiliary variable `LAI` (type `FT`, domain `surface`)
- Functions: `compute_L_max()`, `compute_m()`, `lambertw0()`, `compute_steady_state_LAI()`, `update_LAI!()`
  - Additional parameters (`Ao_annual`, `P_annual`, `D_growing`, `ca`, `GSL`) are optional arguments with defaults in `update_optimal_LAI()`
- Callback mechanism: `call_update_optimal_LAI()`, `make_OptimalLAI_callback()`, `get_model_callbacks()`
- LAI is automatically updated at local noon via the callback system
- Constructor `OptimalLAIParameters{FT}(toml_dict)` reads parameters from TOML configuration

**Restored `src/standalone/Vegetation/pmodel.jl`**
- Removed ~415 lines of LAI code
- Removed `:L` from auxiliary variables
- Reverted to main branch (clean photosynthesis-only implementation)

**Updated `src/standalone/Vegetation/Canopy.jl`**
- Added `lai_model` field to `CanopyModel` struct
- Updated `canopy_components()` to include `:lai_model`
- Updated constructors to accept `lai_model` parameter
- LAI model callbacks are automatically collected with other component callbacks

**Updated diagnostics**
- `land_compute_methods.jl`: Changed path `p.canopy.photosynthesis.L` → `p.canopy.lai_model.LAI`

**Updated `toml/default_parameters.toml`**
- Added optimal LAI model parameters: `optimal_lai_k`, `optimal_lai_z`, `optimal_lai_chi`, `optimal_lai_f0`, `optimal_lai_sigma`, `optimal_lai_alpha`

**Updated `experiments/integrated/fluxnet/ozark_pmodel.jl`**
- Added `OptimalLAIModel` instantiation using TOML parameters
- Passed `lai_model` to `CanopyModel` constructor

## Usage
```julia
# Simple constructor using TOML parameters
lai_model = OptimalLAIModel{FT}(
    OptimalLAIParameters{FT}(toml_dict)
)

# Or with explicit parameters
lai_model = OptimalLAIModel{FT}(
    OptimalLAIParameters{FT}(
        k = 0.5,
        z = 12.227,
        chi = 0.7,
        f0 = 0.62,
        sigma = 0.771,
        alpha = 0.067,
    )
)

# Pass to CanopyModel
canopy = CanopyModel{FT}(
    surface_domain,
    canopy_forcing,
    LAI,
    toml_dict;
    # ... other components ...
    lai_model,
)

# Access LAI via p.canopy.lai_model.LAI
# LAI is automatically updated at local noon through the callback system
```

## Reference
Zhou et al. (2025) "A General Model for the Seasonal to Decadal Dynamics of Leaf Area" Global Change Biology

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> The branch kd/ar/optimal_lai_pmodel adds a prognostic LAI model based on https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1111/gcb.70125 "A General Model for the Seasonal to Decadal Dynamics of Leaf Area" by Zhou et al., 2025 in Global Change Biology. 
> 
> In the branch code, the model is implemented in src/standalone/Vegetation/pmodel.jl. It was tested at a site (experiments/integrated/fluxnet/ozark_pmodel.jl) and works well. I want to move this model to a new file, src/standalone/Vegetation/optimal_lai.jl. pmodel.jl should be back to how it is in main. 
> 
> OptimalLAIModel will be a concrete type of a new AbstractLAIModel abstract type. You will need to define a parameter struct (OptimalLAIParameters) containing k, z, chi, f0, sigma, alpha (you will see them in the equations), and do the other usual thing (look at other models in src/standalone/Vegetation). The auxiliary var is LAI, of type FT, in domain surface. 
> 
> Check that the docstrings look good. 
> 
> Adjust the diagnostics files edit, for example, src/diagnostics/land_compute_methods.jl, L will be stored in a new location with your src change. 
> 
> If something is unclear, let me know. I will try your model (via experiments/integrated/fluxnet/ozark_pmodel.jl) once your PR is ready


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions, customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.
title: Refactor optimal LAI model to standalone module with callback integration

title: use central queue for change to Vast
